hypothesis: If a CVAE trained on Li–Si–X crystal graphs and conditioned on the presence of Si and Li, target capacity >1500 mAh/g, and large Li-channel radius is used to generate candidate materials, then it will produce novel silicon-based anodes whose CHGNet-relaxed structures retain large continuous Li+ migration channels and capacities >1500 mAh/g with Rt nearly unchanged between charged and discharged states and mean energy per atom variation below 4%.
